item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's the point. It's part of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Op it seems that your collapsing block is broken…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the main issue with this one is that it's too short for its own good. There's no time for build-up, to get the reader off-guard - it just goes bang, bang, bang, bang, bang, the turns coming right away and with no subtlety. I'm left feeling like I needed to invest nothing in this, I didn't really have a chance to get disturbed or even confused.

Granted, I'm glad that there are SCPs that aren't sprawling epics, which even if they work can be a bit of a long haul to read, but this one felt like it needed a little more depth to it. Something to get me pulled in before it tried to mess with me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The point is the collapsible has already been opened, and the person writing it is infected. Everything that out of whack there is supposed to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the content, but the article could have the actual dropdown link instead of just the text and the second half needs some tone/grammar adjustments, which makes me a neutral vote on this right now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, I know this case. I remember growing up with Debs. I knew the… thing… the cops brought back wasn't her! Now you make light of the whole situation? My best friend is dead and you're making light of a community in mourning!

+ You are a Monster
- Trolol

I love these fourth wall SCiPs. I always feel like the Foundation works best when we take the "leaked data" approach rather than the "somewhere in the omniverse" approach to suspension of disbelief. This is the first time I've ever +1'd a post

item.title: Falling Short of Potential
item.content:encoded:
Very well written scip; I think the transition from document to "we're going to find our daughter" is particularly well done, so kudos on that.

I was actually a bit disappointed because the premise of "memetic conspiracy which cannot be extracted from the host" can be taken in much, much more interesting places. The twist is fairly easy to see coming, so the idea can be played with in other ways. Personally I would go with records of the Foundation trying desperately to cover up mass protests, vigils, and large-scale consequences of people buying into the conspiracy, or else shed some doubt on the veracity of Deborah's identity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AHA! I HAVE TROLLED YOU! :V

It's supposed to look like a collapsible, but it's already been opened. :) Just text, not actually a block!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Liking it, but is the block at the bottom broken or is it just my phone? I can see the block itself, as well as the open and close texts, but none of them work.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm, I really like this but I have a feeling I don't fully understand. None the less, the execution is good enough for an up vote.

+1

item.title: Re: Author lament
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I did let kaktus know it wasn't working, but of course I didn't find out until I went to post the darn thing. :B

item.title: Re: Author lament
item.content:encoded: We definitely should not encourage that viewpoint, but I thank you for the vote of confidence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a fan. I usually don't go for SCPs that break the fourth wall with the idea that reading them is a "hazard," but this one doesn't seem to beat readers over the head too much with prefaces along the lines of "~ooo it's dangerous~"

One thing I wanted to mention. Even with the Foundation being as powerful as it is, would it really take only six months to (at least mostly) memory-wipe or get rid of 9 million affected people? I dunno, but things would feel realistic if more information was given on how they accomplished that.

item.title: Re: Author lament
item.content:encoded:
I'm gonna coldpost the fuck out of this site, just you watch me!

I feel like this is an attitude we shouldn't encourage, but if all coldposts were as high quality as this, I'd be singing a different tune.

And I've decided it's high time I stop waiting around for my skips to magically get perfect

Damn. I've certainly fallen into that trap. It's even scarier when you (I mean me) haven't actually posted anything yet.

item.title: Re: Author lament
item.content:encoded: I fixed the warning image (linked to a version that's on my author page). I'm on my phone right now, so I didn't read the page just yet; will try to get to it later!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really nice twist on the idea of a changeling. +1

item.title: Author lament
item.content:encoded:
Once again, I bring you memes from fandoms outside the Foundation! This one's probably more obscure, but that's not the point.

The point is SCP-3256 is pretty much the same as this, and way better, and you should probably read that instead. D: But I started writing this back in December, probably? And I've decided it's high time I stop waiting around for my skips to magically get perfect. I'm gonna coldpost the fuck out of this site, just you watch me!

Anyway, thanks to djkaktus for loaning me the warning box at the top. As I write this, the image isn't showing, but that'll probably get sorted out later.
